zh.title: 瑞波币/美元技术分析：短期震荡偏弱整理格局
zh.content:
短期走势呈现震荡偏弱格局，价格1.144运行于20日均线1.17058下方，整体反弹动能不足，市场重心持续在低位附近徘徊，短线仍以整理修正为主导结构。

技术指标方面，MACD为-0.0132持续位于零轴下方运行，空头动能略占优势，虽缺乏RSI参考，但从价格结构看，买盘力量不足，整体动能偏弱信号较为明确。

支撑位关注1.13906日内低点，一旦失守可能进一步下探至1.12附近区域；上方阻力集中在1.15以及1.17058均线位置，形成明显压制带，突破难度较大。

交易策略建议短线以区间思路为主，未有效突破1.15前不宜盲目追多，可等待反弹受阻后轻仓布局空单，止损设于阻力上方，控制风险更为稳妥。

en.title: Ripple vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
is trading in a mild short-term bearish consolidation, with price drifting slightly below the 20-period SMA at 1.17058. The market remains compressed between 1.139 and 1.149, showing limited volatility but a slight downward bias. The failure to reclaim the moving average suggests sellers are gradually dominating momentum, though no strong breakdown has occurred yet. Overall structure remains range-bound but fragile on the downside.

Momentum indicators reinforce a cautious outlook. The price sits below the 20-day SMA at 1.17058, confirming a short-term bearish trend. MACD reading at -0.0132 signals weakening bullish pressure and sustained downside momentum, while the absence of RSI limits precision but aligns with a neutral-to-bearish tilt. Lack of recovery above moving average suggests trend continuation risk remains elevated unless buyers reclaim 1.15–1.17 zone decisively.

Immediate support is defined near 1.13906, the intraday low, with a secondary cushion around 1.1300 if selling pressure accelerates. Resistance appears at 1.14901, followed by a more significant barrier at the 20-day SMA near 1.17058. A breakout above this zone could shift bias toward 1.18. Until then, price action suggests sellers defend rebounds and buyers struggle to sustain upward momentum beyond intraday highs.

Trading conditions favor a range-bound strategy with a bearish bias. Short positions are preferable near resistance zones around 1.149–1.170 with confirmation of rejection. Long setups require clear stabilization above 1.149 and ideally a reclaim of the SMA to target 1.18. Risk control remains essential, with stops below 1.130 to guard against breakdown acceleration. Until a breakout occurs, volatility favors mean-reversion rather than trend-following.
